dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8525 IH: To amend the Specialty Crops Competitiveness Act of 2004 to provide for seasonal and perishable programs, and for other purposes.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Ruiz introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Agriculture

form.official-title: To amend the Specialty Crops Competitiveness Act of 2004 to provide for seasonal and perishable programs, and for other purposes.

section.text: The Specialty Crops Competitiveness Act of 2004 (Public Law 108–465) is amended by adding at the end the following:

section.header: Seasonal and perishable crop loss program

paragraph.text: Beginning with marketing year 2027, and each year thereafter, with respect to a producer of a seasonal and perishable crop in a geographic region described in paragraph (2), the Secretary shall make annual crop loss payments in accordance with this section if the Secretary determines that—

subparagraph.text: the effective price for the seasonal and perishable crop for such marketing year is less than the reference price for such crop; and
the crop loss described in subparagraph (A) is caused by imports of the seasonal and perishable crop.

paragraph.text: Geographic coverage described in this paragraph refers to coverage of a geographic region in the United States—

subparagraph.text: in which a seasonable and perishable commodity is grown within a seasonal marketing window; and
for which an annual normal harvest and shipment of such seasonal and perishable crop occurs.

subsection.text: The effective price for a seasonal and perishable crop shall be the national average market price for the seasonal and perishable crop during the seasonal marketing window.

If seasonal perishable crop loss program payments are required to be provided under this section for the last seasonal marketing window for a covered seasonal and perishable crop, the amount of the seasonal perishable crop loss program payment to be paid to producers on a farm for the seasonal marketing window shall be equal to the product obtained by multiplying—

paragraph.text: the payment rate for the covered seasonal and perishable crop which shall be equal to the difference between—

subparagraph.text: the reference price for the covered seasonal and perishable crop; and
the effective price determined under subsection (b) for the seasonal and perishable crop; and

paragraph.text: the previous three-year average production for the covered seasonal and perishable crop during the same seasonal marketing window.

subsection.text: A producer may only be eligible for a payment under this section if such producer—

paragraph.text: has an average adjusted gross income of less than $5,000,000 for the 3 tax years preceding the most recent tax year; or
derives at least 75 percent of the adjusted gross income of such producer from farming, ranching, or forestry for the tax year for which payment under this section is sought.
The term seasonal marketing window means, with respect to a seasonal and perishable crop—

subparagraph.text: the season of a crop year in which such crop is normally marketed within a geographic region described in subsection (a)(2); and
the season that concludes on the date that is not later than 8 weeks after the last day on which such crops are harvested during a normal marketing window.

paragraph.text: The term reference price means the national average market price received by producers for domestically produced seasonal and perishable crop for the period of the most recent three marketing seasons.
The term seasonal and perishable crop means a crop that is—

subparagraph.text: a fresh or chilled specialty crop that, if imported, would be classified under HS2020 tariff codes 0701, 0702, 0703, 0704, 0705, 0706, 0707, 0708, 0709, 0803, 0804, 0805, 0806, 0807, 0808, 0809, or 0810 (or such successor codes, as determined by the Secretary);
marketed in raw form for consumption without further processing; and
normally marketed not later than 8 weeks after harvesting.

paragraph.text: The term Secretary means the Secretary of Agriculture.
